entries.title: Activists push California schools to teach 'Black English'
entries.description: A Black activist group is urging the California public school system to accommodate "Black English" in schools, arguing that Black children's use of the dialect must be "honored."

entries.title: Judge orders release of more than $5 million due to E. Jean Carroll in Trump sex abuse, defamation case
entries.description: The damages were awarded to Carroll, a writer, by a 2023 jury in a civil sexual abuse and defamation case.

entries.title: White House announces "Freedom Fuel" gas station selling $3.47 gasoline
entries.description: The Freedom Fuel Network is offering gasoline for more than 30 cents a gallon below the national average.

entries.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list
entries.description: The nominations for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards were announced Wednesday morning in Los Angeles, with the final season of HBO Max's "Hacks" setting a new record for the most nominations in a single year for a comedy series.

entries.title: Former Wisconsin judge spared prison for obstructing ICE arrest of Mexican immigrant
entries.description: Former Wisconsin Judge Hannah Dugan was spared from prison Wednesday for ushering a Mexican defendant out of her courtroom to evade U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents. A federal judge fined her $5,000 and cited her otherwise law-abiding life in issuing the sentence.

entries.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
entries.description: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: Two teens detained in California after drinking, shooting toy guns out of Waymo robotaxi
entries.description: Waymo halted one of its robotaxis mid-ride and called California cops after it caught two teen passengers drinking booze and shooting pellets from toy guns, police said.

entries.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal until 2027 after latest setback, Kalshi traders predict
entries.description: Speculators now see just a 43% chance that traffic flows return to normal by Dec. 1.

entries.title: Fed officials were split on direction of interest rates at last meeting, minutes show

entries.title: Family demands an independent probe after ICE officer fatally shoots a man in Houston
entries.description: A Mexican national fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer in Houston had no criminal convictions during his decades living in the U.S. and was driving a crew to a homebuilding site when he was killed, his family and a Texas congresswoman said Wednesday.

entries.title: What to know about protecting pets from the New World screwworm fly
entries.description: Two New World screwworm cases in dogs are among more than 30 confirmed instances in Texas and New Mexico, prompting warnings Wednesday from veterinarians and humane societies that pet owners need to remain vigilant to protect their animals.

entries.title: The Trump administration is ramping up pressure on states to change election practices
entries.description: President Donald Trump's administration is threatening to withhold some federal funding from states that don't make changes to voting practices and is warning state election officials that they face arrest if they don't remove noncitizens from voter rolls.

entries.title: Man banned from national parks in Arizona after living illegally for years in Tonto National Forest

entries.title: Canada's Carney visits Saudi Arabia as the prime minister seeks to expand ties with kingdom

entries.title: What is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?
entries.description: Illness surging in Michigan and other US states is rarely life-threatening, CDC says – but it can have severe effectsCases of cyclosporiasis – a parasitic illness that can cause “explosive”, watery diarrhea – have surged across the United States in recent days, health officials have said, with an abnormally large outbreak of almost 1,000 cases reported in Michigan.Michigan typically reports about 50 cases a year, making the current outbreak the largest in the state’s history and one of the nation’s biggest in recent years. Ohio has also reported a sharp increase, with 177 cases as of 2 July, since the CDC’s last count. Continue reading...

entries.title: Legionnaires’ outbreak rocks New York as experts warn of rising climate threat
entries.description: Officials say climate crisis ‘worsening our exposure’ to bacteria as at least 28 people sickened in ManhattanA New York outbreak of legionnaires’ disease, a rare but severe form of pneumonia, highlights the microbe’s growing and disproportionate impacts in a warming climate.At least 28 people have been sickened in an outbreak on the Upper East Side, a wealthy neighborhood between Central Park and the East River in Manhattan. Health department officials, seeking to stop the outbreak, have sampled water from nearly 160 building cooling towers to test for the bacteria. Continue reading...
